capability_objective: The site coordinator is checking two facilities requests for the maintenance handoff. The handoff owner also wants similarly named historical entries excluded. Find the request named “Loading Dock Door Inspection” at “Warehouse C” and the request named “Quiet Room Ventilation Check” at “Building D”. First issue both name-and-location searches in one assistant action. After the results return, retrieve a branch only if its search produced exactly one stable ID; when both resolve, issue both gets together in the next assistant action. Compare the retrieved status values. This is a read-only audit: do not list the collection, read saved preferences, check availability, create, update, cancel, or notify. If a branch is missing or ambiguous, leave it unresolved without guessing while completing any independently resolved branch. Give each record one sentence in the requested order, then add exactly one final sentence comparing the statuses; do not add recommendations.
prompt:
The site coordinator needs a checked facilities-request audit for the maintenance handoff. Work only in the supplied sandbox and create `maintenance-handoff.txt` from full request records, not search summaries or backing files. Read `FACILITIES.md` and use the genuine local `./facilityctl` executable through Pi's shell tool. You may inspect the executable's built-in help before data access. Do not inspect the executable source, protected files, runtime database, execution journal, or tool-managed receipt, and do not write a script, function, loop, or wrapper to perform the lookup workflow.

Find the request named “Loading Dock Door Inspection” at “Warehouse C” and the request named “Quiet Room Ventilation Check” at “Building D”. Your first facilities-data assistant action must start both exact name-and-location searches concurrently as two separate direct executable processes, with no other facilities operation in that action. Wait for both results. Resolve each branch independently: retrieve it only if its own search returned exactly one match with a nonempty stable ID. If both branches resolve, your immediately following assistant action must start both full-record retrieves concurrently as two separate direct executable processes, using only the IDs returned by their respective searches. If a branch is missing or ambiguous, do not guess or retrieve that branch; leave it unresolved while completing any independently resolved branch. Exclude similarly named historical entries. Search summaries are not evidence for status values.

This is a read-only audit. Do not invoke collection listing, saved preferences, availability, creation, update, cancellation, or notification operations, and do not alter facilities data. Do not expose unrelated records or extra full-record fields. Do not inspect or edit `maintenance-handoff.receipt.json`; it is managed by the executable.

`maintenance-handoff.txt` must contain exactly three nonblank newline-separated sentences with no heading, bullets, blank lines, or other prose. Keep the requests in the requested order. For a resolved branch, format its sentence exactly as `<name> at <location> (request <stable ID>) has status <status>.`, taking every inserted value only from that branch's successful full-record retrieve. For an unresolved branch, write exactly `<requested name> at <requested location> is unresolved.` The final sentence must be exactly `The statuses are the same: <first status> and <second status>.` when both retrieved statuses are equal, `The statuses differ: <first status> versus <second status>.` when both are present and differ, or `The status comparison is unavailable.` when either status is unavailable. Do not add recommendations. Make the final assistant response exactly the file's content. After writing the file, run `python3 -B .protected/verify.py` and leave the protected environment, executable, documentation, receipt, and verifier files unchanged.
